private_memo:
# Morning memo — 2026-06-18

**Source failures (if any):** None apparent

## Worth attention

1. **SpaceX to buy Cursor (Anysphere) for $60B** — https://www.reuters.com/legal/transactional/spacex-buy-anysphere-60-billion-2026-06-16/
   Reuters reports SpaceX is acquiring Anysphere (the company behind Cursor) for $60B. A seismic shift for AI-assisted coding — if you rely on Cursor, watch for changes to roadmap, pricing, or access under SpaceX ownership.

2. **Running local models is good now** — https://vickiboykis.com/2026/06/15/running-local-models-is-good-now/
   Vicki Boykis argues local LLMs have crossed a quality threshold for practical daily work. Directly relevant if you run Ollama — you may no longer need to compromise much on quality vs. cloud APIs for most tasks.

3. **Stop Using JWTs** — https://gist.github.com/samsch/0d1f3d3b4745d778f78b230cf6061452
   Detailed argument for server-side sessions over JWTs: JWTs add complexity without meaningful benefit for most web apps and make revocation harder. Worth reading before any auth decisions on CalenCall or ContractorVerify.

4. **Google Chrome ending popular ad blockers** — https://9to5google.com/2026/06/15/google-chromes-next-update-will-mark-the-end-of-popular-ad-blockers/
   Chrome's next update completes Manifest V3, disabling extensions like uBlock Origin that relied on the old blocking API. Switch to uBlock Origin Lite or Firefox now if you rely on ad blocking for development or privacy.

5. **Backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer** — https://roman.pt/posts/linkedin-backdoor/
   A developer received a fake job offer via LinkedIn containing a link to a backdoored coding challenge repo. Social engineering via job offers is an active attack vector — treat unsolicited repo links from recruiters as hostile.

6. **Memory safety CVEs differ between Rust and C/C++** — https://kobzol.github.io/rust/2026/06/15/how-memory-safety-cves-differ-between-rust-and-c-cpp.html
   Research showing Rust changes the nature of memory CVEs, not just the count — Rust eliminates corruption bugs but logic-level issues remain. Useful context for evaluating security claims about Rust rewrites.

7. **datasette 1.0a34 — row editing in the UI** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/datasette/#atom-everything
   Datasette adds insert, edit, and delete row UI in this alpha. Moves it closer to a lightweight database admin tool. If you use datasette for data browsing on any project, this is worth an upgrade.

8. **Claude API — three elevated-error incidents Jun 16-17** — https://status.claude.com/incidents/xmhsglsz3h3w
   Anthropic logged three separate error-rate incidents in two days affecting Sonnet and Opus (all resolved). Notable reliability pattern if you're building on Claude's API — consider retry/fallback logic.

## Full digest
- [P] [hn-top] SpaceX to buy Cursor for $60B — https://www.reuters.com/legal/transactional/spacex-buy-anysphere-60-billion-2026-06-16/ — Major acquisition changes AI coding tool landscape
- [P] [lobsters] Running local models is good now — https://vickiboykis.com/2026/06/15/running-local-models-is-good-now/ — Local LLMs now practical for daily dev work
- [P] [hn-top] Stop Using JWTs — https://gist.github.com/samsch/0d1f3d3b4745d778f78b230cf6061452 — Argument for sessions over JWTs for most web auth
- [P] [lobsters] Chrome ending ad blockers — https://9to5google.com/2026/06/15/google-chromes-next-update-will-mark-the-end-of-popular-ad-blockers/ — Manifest V3 kills extension-based ad blocking
- [P] [lobsters] Backdoor in LinkedIn job offer — https://roman.pt/posts/linkedin-backdoor/ — Malicious code repo sent via fake LinkedIn recruiter
- [P] [lobsters] Memory safety CVEs Rust vs C/C++ — https://kobzol.github.io/rust/2026/06/15/how-memory-safety-cves-differ-between-rust-and-c-cpp.html — Rust changes bug types, not just reduces count
- [P] [simon-willison] datasette 1.0a34 — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/datasette/#atom-everything — Row insert/edit/delete now in datasette UI
- [P] [claude-status] Claude API repeated errors Jun 16-17 — https://status.claude.com/incidents/xmhsglsz3h3w — Three incidents, all resolved; pattern worth noting
- [M] [lobsters] zlib-rs in Firefox — https://trifectatech.org/blog/zlib-rs-in-firefox/ — Rust zlib landing in Firefox
- [M] [simon-willison] Georgi Gerganov on Qwen3.6-27B — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/georgi-gerganov/#atom-everything — llama.cpp creator endorses Qwen3.6-27B for local coding
- [M] [openai-blog] OpenAI Deployment Simulation — https://openai.com/index/deployment-simulation — Pre-release model behavior prediction via simulated deployment
- [R] [hn-top] Stop Killing Games fails EU law — off-topic
- [R] [hn-top] Yak shaving is fun (2019) — old, no signal
- [R] [hn-top] PDP-11 tour (2022) — historical, not actionable
- [R] [hn-top] 10Gb/s Ethernet — home networking, not relevant
- [R] [lobsters] Firefox what's next — general roadmap, no signal
- [R] [simon-willison] click-to-play web component — niche tool
- [R] [simon-willison] datasette-tailscale 0.1a0 — too early, too niche
- [R] [claude-status] Opus 4.8 errors Jun 17 — resolved, covered above
- [R] [claude-status] Opus 4.8 brief errors Jun 16 — resolved, covered above

results.title: Stop Using JWTs
results.summary: A detailed technical argument for replacing JWTs with server-side sessions in web apps. JWTs add complexity (revocation, key rotation, token bloat) without meaningful benefit for typical use cases. Sessions are simpler, safer, and easier to reason about for standard web auth.
results.raw_claim: JWTs are unnecessarily complex for most web authentication use cases; server-side sessions are simpler and more secure.
results.uncertainty: No content was fetched; assessment based on title and well-known prior discussion of this article.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly relevant to auth decisions in CalenCall and ContractorVerify.
results.verdict_reason: Classic, high-signal JWT critique relevant to any dev making auth decisions.

results.title: Stop Killing Games fails to secure EU law despite 1.3M signatures
results.summary: The Stop Killing Games initiative, which aimed to require publishers to maintain offline playability after service shutdown, failed to secure EU legislation despite 1.3M signatures.
results.raw_claim: Stop Killing Games initiative failed to secure EU legislation despite 1.3M signatures.
results.verdict_reason: Gaming industry/EU policy news not relevant to solo software developer audience.

results.title: SpaceX to buy Cursor (Anysphere) for $60B
results.summary: Reuters reports SpaceX is acquiring Anysphere, the company behind Cursor AI coding assistant, for $60B. One of the largest acquisitions in AI tooling history. The deal raises immediate questions about Cursor's future roadmap, pricing, and independence under SpaceX ownership.
results.raw_claim: SpaceX is acquiring Anysphere (the company behind Cursor AI coding assistant) for $60 billion.
results.uncertainty: Deal may not have closed; implications for existing Cursor users are unknown.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly affects anyone using Cursor for AI-assisted coding.
results.verdict_reason: Major acquisition of leading AI coding tool; any developer using or evaluating Cursor needs to know.

results.title: But yak shaving is fun (2019)
results.summary: A 2019 blog post arguing that yak shaving is actually enjoyable and part of the creative process of software development.
results.raw_claim: Yak shaving is an enjoyable and productive part of software development, not merely a distraction.
results.verdict_reason: Old article (2019), no new signal, entertainment content.

results.title: A brief tour of the PDP-11 (2022)
results.summary: A 2022 Ars Technica retrospective on the PDP-11 minicomputer and its influence on Unix and modern computing architecture.
results.raw_claim: The PDP-11 was among the most influential minicomputers in history, shaping Unix and modern computing architecture.
results.verdict_reason: Historical computing content from 2022, no actionable signal.

results.title: 10Gb/s Ethernet: switching to a Broadcom SFP+ module
results.summary: A home lab networking post about upgrading to 10Gb/s Ethernet using a Broadcom SFP+ module.
results.raw_claim: Broadcom SFP+ modules can be used for 10Gb/s home networking upgrades with appropriate configuration.
results.verdict_reason: Home networking hardware post, not relevant to software development work.

results.title: Google Chrome's next update will mark the end of popular ad blockers
results.summary: Chrome's upcoming update completes the Manifest V3 transition, disabling extensions like uBlock Origin that relied on the deprecated blocking API. Developers relying on ad blockers for privacy or cleaner dev browsing should switch to uBlock Origin Lite or migrate to Firefox.
results.raw_claim: Chrome's next update completes Manifest V3, ending support for the blocking API used by popular ad blockers like uBlock Origin.
results.uncertainty: Exact Chrome version and rollout date not available from content snippet.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Affects developer tooling and privacy workflows for anyone using Chrome.
results.verdict_reason: Affects day-to-day browser tooling for developers; actionable switch needed.

results.title: See what's next for Firefox
results.summary: Mozilla published a roadmap page for upcoming Firefox features and improvements.
results.raw_claim: Mozilla has published a roadmap of upcoming Firefox features and improvements.
results.uncertainty: No specific features available from content snippet.
results.verdict_reason: General browser roadmap marketing page, no specific actionable signal.

results.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
results.summary: A developer documents receiving a fake job offer via LinkedIn directing them to a GitHub repository containing backdoored code disguised as a coding challenge. An active social engineering attack pattern targeting developers via unsolicited job contact.
results.raw_claim: A developer received a LinkedIn job offer containing a link to a backdoored coding challenge repository designed to compromise their system.
results.uncertainty: Details of exact payload not confirmed from content snippet.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Active attack pattern targeting developers via LinkedIn; directly actionable security awareness.
results.verdict_reason: Concrete, documented security attack vector targeting developers specifically.

results.title: zlib-rs in Firefox
results.summary: A Rust implementation of zlib (zlib-rs) is being integrated into Firefox, replacing the C implementation for memory safety improvements without performance regression.
results.raw_claim: Firefox is integrating zlib-rs, a Rust implementation of the zlib compression library, to replace its C implementation.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Signal in the ongoing Rust-in-browsers trend; relevant to infrastructure and security tracking.
results.verdict_reason: Interesting Rust ecosystem signal, not directly actionable for solo dev work.

results.title: How memory safety CVEs differ between Rust and C/C++
results.summary: Research showing Rust and C/C++ produce qualitatively different memory safety vulnerabilities — Rust eliminates memory corruption CVEs but logic-level issues remain. Challenges the simple claim that Rust eliminates all memory safety bugs.
results.raw_claim: Rust eliminates traditional memory corruption CVEs but produces different types of memory safety bugs versus C/C++; the languages have different CVE profiles, not just different counts.
results.uncertainty: Methodology details and dataset size not available from content snippet.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Useful calibration for evaluating Rust security claims in tech stack decisions.
results.verdict_reason: Well-evidenced research that calibrates the Rust-for-safety narrative; useful for tech stack decisions.

results.title: Running local models is good now
results.summary: Vicki Boykis argues local LLMs have crossed a quality threshold in 2026 where they are genuinely useful for daily development work without significant compromise versus cloud APIs. Relevant to developers running Ollama locally — the gap has narrowed substantially.
results.raw_claim: Local LLMs have reached a quality level in 2026 where they are genuinely useful for daily development work without significant quality compromise versus cloud APIs.
results.uncertainty: Specific models and benchmarks cited in the article not available from content snippet.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly relevant to Ollama usage decisions and when to use local vs. API models.
results.verdict_reason: Practical quality signal for local model users; directly informs Ollama workflow and API spend decisions.

results.title: <click-to-play> — a Web Component for on-demand GIF playback
results.summary: Simon Willison released a Web Component that wraps GIF links to show a still frame with a play button, loading the full GIF only on click.
results.raw_claim: A new Web Component wraps GIF links to show a static first frame with a play button, loading the full GIF only on demand.
results.verdict_reason: Useful micro-tool but too niche and low-impact for the audience.

results.title: datasette 1.0a34 — row editing in the UI
results.summary: Datasette 1.0a34 adds UI tools for inserting, editing, and deleting rows directly in the browser interface on table and row pages. Moves datasette from a read-only browser toward a lightweight database admin tool.
results.raw_claim: Datasette 1.0a34 adds row insert, edit, and delete capabilities directly in the web UI.
results.uncertainty: Still alpha; API stability not guaranteed.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Significant feature for datasette users; turns it into a usable admin UI.
results.verdict_reason: Meaningful feature addition to a widely used data tool; actionable for datasette users.

results.title: datasette-tailscale 0.1a0
results.summary: An experimental alpha plugin that integrates Tailscale with Datasette, serving a datasette instance directly on your Tailnet with a single command.
results.raw_claim: datasette-tailscale 0.1a0 is an experimental plugin that runs datasette on a Tailscale network using a sidecar daemon.
results.uncertainty: Very early alpha; may change significantly.
results.verdict_reason: Too early (alpha), too niche (datasette + Tailscale intersection), not actionable.

results.title: Georgi Gerganov endorses Qwen3.6-27B for local coding tasks
results.summary: Georgi Gerganov (creator of llama.cpp) states he has been using Qwen3.6-27B daily on M2 Ultra and RTX 5090 for small coding tasks over the past month. A strong practitioner endorsement for local coding model selection.
results.raw_claim: Georgi Gerganov (llama.cpp creator) has been using Qwen3.6-27B daily for coding tasks and endorses it as a capable local coding model.
results.uncertainty: Tested on high-end hardware (M2 Ultra, RTX 5090); performance on more modest hardware may differ.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Strong signal from credible practitioner about local coding model quality; relevant to Ollama usage.
results.verdict_reason: Strong practitioner endorsement of a specific local model; held to monitor to avoid two publish items from simon-willison.

results.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.8 (Jun 17)
results.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 experienced elevated errors on June 17, 2026 from 9:59pm to 10:41pm PT. Resolved. Covered by the larger multi-model incident.
results.raw_claim: Claude Opus 4.8 had elevated API errors on June 17, 2026 between 9:59pm and 10:41pm PT, now resolved.
results.verdict_reason: Resolved incident; covered by the broader multi-model incident entry.

results.title: Opus 4.8 brief errors (Jun 16)
results.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 had briefly elevated error rates on June 16, 2026 from 12:41 to 12:53 PDT. Resolved. Covered by the larger multi-model incident.
results.raw_claim: Claude Opus 4.8 had a brief elevated error rate on June 16, 2026 from 12:41 to 12:53 PDT, now resolved.
results.verdict_reason: Brief resolved incident; covered by the broader multi-model incident entry.

results.title: Claude API — elevated errors across Sonnet and Opus (Jun 16-17)
results.summary: Anthropic logged three separate error-rate incidents in two days affecting Sonnet and Opus models, with the June 16 incident reaching ~10% error rate for nearly two hours. All resolved, but the pattern is notable for reliability-sensitive Claude API users.
results.raw_claim: Claude API experienced three separate elevated-error incidents on June 16-17, 2026 affecting Sonnet and Opus models, with error rates reaching ~10% during the June 16 incident.
results.uncertainty: Root cause not disclosed in status updates.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Pattern of instability relevant to Claude API users building reliability-sensitive features.
results.verdict_reason: Three incidents in two days is a reliability signal worth noting for Claude API users.

results.title: OpenAI Deployment Simulation — predicting model behavior before release
results.summary: OpenAI published research on 'Deployment Simulation,' using real historical conversation data to predict model behavior and safety properties before release. Aims to catch safety regressions pre-deployment.
results.raw_claim: OpenAI introduced Deployment Simulation, a pre-release evaluation method using real historical conversation data to predict model behavior and safety properties before deployment.
results.uncertainty: Whether this changes existing model release timelines is unclear.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Signal on how frontier labs approach model safety evaluation at scale.
results.verdict_reason: Interesting AI safety research but no near-term actionable decision impact for a solo builder.
